page: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor. It features 96 physical cores and 96 vCPUs with a dedicated allocation, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache per core. It is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs. Network connectivity is supported by a 40 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top tier across almost all performance metrics. The single-core stress-ng score of 3776.89 and multi-core score of 349774.63 demonstrate high raw processing power. Memory bandwidth is efficient, reaching 16842.02 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 518.29 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The system also achieves 243.25 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 111.85 GB/sec in static web serving throughput. For large language model inference, it processes prompts at 39.58 tokens/sec and generates text at 9.81 tokens/sec using a 70B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c8a.metal-24xl offers high resource density for compute-heavy workloads, though the lack of bundled local storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff for storage-intensive or hardware-accelerated tasks. The absence of hyperthreading ensures predictable, dedicated performance per core. This configuration is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, high-performance computing, cryptography, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server featuring 96 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 192.0 GB of system memory, 40 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks place this server in the top tier for raw CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations. It is highly suited for compute-intensive workloads, including software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8a.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server with 96 AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB RAM, and 40 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier CPU and memory performance.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
